messages.content: network request on my chrome do not see payload tab

If you are using Chrome DevTools and unable to see the "Payload" tab for network requests, it means that you are likely looking at a request that does not have a payload associated with it.

The "Payload" tab in Chrome DevTools is only visible for requests that contain a payload. A payload refers to the data that is sent as part of a request, such as the body of a POST or PUT request, or even query parameters.

If you are looking at a request and there is no payload tab available, it indicates that the request does not contain any data payload. In such cases, you may see other tabs like "Headers", "Preview", "Response", etc., depending on the available data for that particular request.

To verify if a request has a payload or not, you can check the request method (e.g., GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) and the data being sent along with the request. If it does not include any data, the payload tab will not be available.

However, if you believe there should be a payload associated with the request and you are still unable to see it, make sure you are not filtering the network requests or using any browser extensions or tools that might affect the DevTools functionality.
